title: Phonk-Energy 9:16 Reel/TikTok Edit Package — Sizzle Cut, Reframe, Captions, and Scroll-Stopping Thumbnail
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the real brief: raw vertical footage in; deliver 1x final 9:16 MP4 ready for Instagram & TikTok; on-screen text/captions; an exported thumbnail frame; a polished/professional look with on-brand color work; phonk-style energy; 15-45s runtime; HD/Full HD; 24-48h turnaround on a lean budget. ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark (and to stay inside the capability sheet's honest boundaries): (1) the brief's "trimming + smooth transitions + motion graphics + tasteful text animations" is a Premiere-class timeline edit that NO Adobe connector tool performs — so the spec re-scopes those words to what the suite actually offers: video_create_quick_cut [A] is the AI sizzle/highlight assembly (visual-engagement, the connector's nearest analog to "pick the best parts + tight pacing"), and video_resize [A] is the 9:16 platform reframe (same length, no trim). The spec states plainly that frame-accurate cuts, hard transitions, and animated motion graphics are NOT connector-doable and are flagged as the one human-finish step. (2) The "exported thumbnail frame" is realized as a full headless [C] raster pipeline on a client-supplied hero still grabbed from the footage — straighten, auto-tone, phonk color grade (cool teal/magenta), subject pop, halftone-grain texture, crop to the exact 1080x1920 cover, then a vectorized logo lockup — which is where the bulk of the genuine output-to-input Adobe chain lives. (3) Captions are authored as an on-screen text/caption asset uploaded to CC for the editor; the connector cannot burn text onto video frames. Nothing in the A6 not-available list is used as a core deliverable.
one_line_ask: Turn one batch of raw vertical clips plus a hero still, brand logo, and a caption script into a post-ready 9:16 phonk-energy short: an AI sizzle cut reframed to 1080x1920, an on-screen caption sheet, and a fully color-graded exported thumbnail with a vector logo lockup.
full_brief: I run a fast-growing Instagram + TikTok presence and need a short-form edit shipped post-ready for both platforms. For this assignment I'm sending: (a) three raw vertical clips from a night drive / street shoot, (b) one hero still grabbed from the footage to become the cover/thumbnail, (c) my brand wordmark logo as a flat PNG, and (d) a captions script as data. The concept is phonk-style energy but with a professional, polished finish — clean, current, on-brand color (cool teal shadows, magenta highlight bite). Deliverables: 1x final ~30s 9:16 MP4 ready to post on Instagram and TikTok (HD/Full HD); a matching on-screen captions/text asset; and an exported, fully color-graded 1080x1920 thumbnail frame with my logo locked up in the corner as a crisp vector so it scales. Source assets stay in my Creative Cloud folder so tweaks are easy. Turnaround 24-48h, lean budget — efficient workflow over flashy effects. NOTE up front: frame-accurate timeline cuts, animated motion graphics, and hard transitions are a manual Premiere finish and are out of scope for the automated connector pass; the automated pass delivers the AI sizzle assembly, the 9:16 reframe, the captions asset, and the graded thumbnail — the human editor does the final timeline polish on top.

inputs.name: raw_clip_nightdrive_01.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Handheld vertical 9:16 phone footage, 1080x1920, ~12 seconds, night drive through a neon-lit city street, dashboard and windshield reflections, passing streetlights streaking, slight handheld shake, real motion blur, candid documentary look, no captions or text overlays, not glossy CGI
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like genuine phone footage: rolling-shutter wobble, real headlight bloom, compression noise in shadows. Visually distinct night-drive motion so the sizzle assembly has strong high-engagement moments to select.

inputs.name: raw_clip_nightdrive_02.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Handheld vertical 9:16 phone footage, 1080x1920, ~10 seconds, subject in hoodie walking under an underpass at night, sodium and neon mixed lighting, breath visible, gritty urban texture, candid, real grain, no text overlays
inputs.realism_notes: Mixed color-temperature lighting (sodium orange vs neon magenta) so the color-grade chain has something real to correct toward the teal/magenta phonk look.

inputs.name: raw_clip_nightdrive_03.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Vertical 9:16 phone footage, 1080x1920, ~9 seconds, slow push-in on rain-slick asphalt reflecting neon signage, shallow depth of field, droplets on lens, moody, candid, no text
inputs.realism_notes: Gives the quick_cut engine visually distinct high-engagement moments (reflections, droplets) to select among the three clips.

inputs.name: hero_cover_still.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal vertical still, 1080x1920 portrait, a person in a hoodie half-lit by neon under a city underpass at night, slightly off-level horizon (tilted ~3 degrees as if grabbed from handheld footage), real skin pores and individual hair flyaways, mixed warm-and-cool street lighting, slight motion blur in background, candid documentary night photography, no text, no logo
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately tilted and mixed-WB so image_auto_straighten, image_apply_auto_tone, and the color-temperature/grade chain all have real work to do. A clear human subject so image_select_subject / Subject-Pop preset are meaningful.

inputs.name: brand_wordmark_logo.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat minimalist wordmark logo on transparent background, the single word 'NOCTURNE' in a tight geometric sans-serif, solid white glyphs, crisp clean edges, high contrast, centered, 2000x600 PNG with alpha, no photographic texture, no gradient
inputs.realism_notes: Flat high-contrast solid-color art so image_vectorize produces a clean SVG; transparent alpha so it composites as a corner lockup on the thumbnail. Text-bearing → gemini-3-pro-image.

inputs.name: captions_script.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a small JSON array of 6 caption cues for a ~30s vertical reel, each cue with fields {index, start_seconds, end_seconds, text}, short punchy on-screen lines matching a moody night-drive phonk mood, all-caps style text, under 24 characters per line
inputs.realism_notes: Realistic caption-cue structure the editor uploads as the on-screen text asset; gives a concrete captions deliverable without claiming the connector can burn text into video.

outputs.name: phonk_sizzle_9x16.mp4
outputs.spec: Final AI sizzle/highlight assembly reframed to vertical 9:16, MP4 (H.264), 1080x1920, HD/Full HD, ~30s target runtime (best-effort — the sizzle assembly and reframe are [A] async product actions whose exact final duration/spec are entitlement/widget-polled and NOT fully headless-retrievable, so ~30s is a target rather than a hard-verifiable value). Produced by video_create_quick_cut (step 22) then video_resize (step 23), same length no trim. Ready to post on Instagram and TikTok. Frame-accurate cuts, hard transitions, and animated motion graphics are the flagged manual Premiere finish and are NOT part of this automated deliverable.

outputs.name: reel_captions.json
outputs.spec: On-screen captions/text asset: a JSON cue sheet of 6 caption cues, each {index, start_seconds, end_seconds, text} in short all-caps night-drive phonk lines (<24 chars/line), finalized into the client's CC project folder (step 25). Delivered as an uploaded data asset for the editor to burn onto the video during the manual finish — the connector does not composite text onto video frames.

outputs.name: thumbnail_cover_1080x1920.png
outputs.spec: Exported, fully color-graded vertical cover/thumbnail, PNG 1080x1920: straightened, auto-toned, phonk-graded (cool teal shadows + magenta highlight bite), subject-popped, halftone-grain texture, subject-aware cropped/reframed to exact 9:16 from the 1080x1920 hero still (a straight reframe of the existing frame — no canvas extension or padding). Designed to carry the brand wordmark as a crisp corner lockup; the actual logo placement/compositing is the flagged human-finish step (the connector chain produces this graded/cropped cover without the logo burned in).

connector_workflow.note: Client supplies the hero still; initialize a block upload of hero_cover_still.png into the client's Creative Cloud cloud-content folder (media_type image/png) to start the project. Returns the transfer document + presigned block URL.
Finalize the block upload from step 1 (pass back its filename + transfer_document) so the hero still is a real CC asset with a fetchable URL to drive the raster chain.
Level the ~3-degree tilt from the handheld grab. Consumes the finalized hero-still URL from step 2.
Neutralize the mixed-WB night exposure as a clean base. Consumes step 3's output URL.
Push shadows cool/teal for the phonk look. Consumes step 4's output URL.
Targeted HSL: deepen teal shadows and bring magenta/pink bite into the highlights for the on-brand phonk grade. Consumes step 5's output URL.
Auto-select the hoodie subject to drive a selective pop. Consumes step 6's graded image and returns a maskURI.
Apply vibrance/saturation lift only within the subject mask from step 7 (subject pop) on the graded image from step 6, so the figure separates from the neon background.
Add a tasteful halftone/print-screen texture for the gritty phonk feel. Consumes step 8's output URL.
Layer fine film grain over the halftone for the finished moody surface. Consumes step 9's output URL.
Subject-aware crop to the exact 1080x1920 (9:16) cover frame, keeping the figure on a rule-of-thirds line. Consumes step 10's output URL.
Bring the client's flat brand wordmark (brand_wordmark_logo.png) into CC via a block upload so it can be vectorized for the lockup.
Finalize the logo upload from step 12 into a real CC asset URL.

Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG from step 13 into a clean scalable SVG (NOCTURNE_logo.svg) for the corner lockup. The thumbnail+logo composite lockup is the one human-finish placement step, since the connector does not composite.
Pull the graded thumbnail (step 11) and the vector logo (step 14) back as base64 to visually QC the grade, crop, and that the wordmark reads crisp before handoff.
Upload raw_clip_nightdrive_01.mp4 (media_type video/mp4) into the project folder to feed the video chain.
Finalize clip 01 into a real CC video assetId.
Upload raw_clip_nightdrive_02.mp4 into the project folder.
Finalize clip 02 into a real CC video assetId.
Upload raw_clip_nightdrive_03.mp4 into the project folder.
Finalize clip 03 into a real CC video assetId. Steps 16-21 give the quick_cut tool its three assetIds.

AI sizzle/highlight assembly across the three finalized clip assetIds (steps 17/19/21), target_duration ~30s, user_prompt for tight-pacing phonk energy. [A] async — the product widget polls and notifies; this is the connector's analog to 'pick the best parts'. Frame-accurate cuts/transitions/motion-graphics remain the flagged manual finish.
Reframe/resize the sizzle cut from step 22 to a clean full-frame 1080x1920 (9:16), SAME length (no trim). [A] async. Produces phonk_sizzle_9x16.mp4.
Upload the captions_script.json cue sheet (media_type application/json) into the project folder as the on-screen text/captions asset for the editor.
Finalize the captions cue sheet (reel_captions.json) into the client's CC project folder so source/captions stay with the project alongside the other finalized deliverables.

chaining_note: One continuous output-to-input chain anchored on the client-supplied assets. The hero still is uploaded (1) then finalized (2); the finalized URL feeds straighten (3) -> auto_tone (4) -> color_temperature (5) -> hsl grade (6). The graded image (6) is consumed by select_subject (7) to make a mask, and both the graded image (6) and that mask (7) feed the masked vibrance/saturation subject-pop (8) -> halftone (9) -> grain (10) -> crop_and_resize to the 1080x1920 thumbnail (11). In parallel the logo is uploaded (12)/finalized (13) and vectorized (14); the thumbnail (11) plus logo SVG (14) are QC'd via inline_preview (15). The three clips are uploaded/finalized (16-21) and consumed by quick_cut (22), whose sizzle output feeds video_resize to the 9:16 reel (23). Captions are uploaded/finalized (24-25) into the client's CC project folder alongside the graded thumbnail (11), vector logo (14), and reframed reel (23) so source and deliverables sit together. Every post-load step sets inputs_from to the producing step's output name; the deliverable package emerges from the chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 25 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools, across 3 groups (A1 raster/grade, A4 asset, A5 video). The raster grade-and-thumbnail spine is a genuine 9-step headless [C] chain with a real masked selective edit (select_subject -> masked vibrance). It is honestly mode-tagged: the video sizzle/reframe is [A] async (entitled, product-widget-polled, not headless-retrievable), and the spec states up front that frame-accurate cuts, transitions, and motion graphics are NOT connector-doable and are the flagged manual Premiere finish — so nothing from the A6 not-available list is claimed as a core deliverable. Difficulty comes from breadth, the strict output->input dependency, mixing [C] and [A] modes, and faithfully re-scoping a Premiere-class brief onto the real Adobe connector surface. The only non-connector composite (logo lockup placement) is named as the single human-finish step, consistent with the 'partial' grade for the timeline polish.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability used. The thumbnail is produced via image_crop_and_resize (gen-free crop/reframe, output <= source dims) — no generative-expand/outpaint, no upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no object-removal-with-reconstruction. image_apply_halftone/image_add_grain are texture filters; image_select_subject + masked image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation is a real selective edit. image_vectorize traces a client-supplied flat PNG. asset_inline_preview is used only for QC, not to emit a subset file. Video steps (video_create_quick_cut, video_resize same-length) are honest [A] async product actions; captions delivered as an uploaded JSON data asset, not burned into video; logo compositing named as the single human-finish step.
reverify.notes: All 25 steps are real Adobe-connector actions with a coherent output->input chain. Only stale over-claim wording (thumbnail 'carries' the lockup) was corrected to match the connector reality (placement is human-finish). No other field described a banned capability.
